claim: CAK's current runtime core is intentionally pure and does not execute scripts or perform I/O.
evidence_quote_or_paraphrase: Runtime v0 is a pure deterministic function from EvalRequest to Decision and is not a script executor, MCP server, markdown parser, or harness.

claim: The Rust core currently has a small dependency surface.
evidence_quote_or_paraphrase: cak-runtime-core depends on serde, serde_json, and thiserror; CLI/adapter depend on clap/anyhow and the core.
notes: Embedding Wasmtime in core would be a major dependency change.

claim: CAK's own RDR scope separates ordinary skill packages from runtime-control skills.
evidence_quote_or_paraphrase: Package-only, script-only, and plugin-only examples are not runtime-control skills unless they have activation, state/action access, loop position, intervention behavior, audit, verifier/admission, and lifecycle.
notes: WASM only matters if CAK permits executable runtime-control artifacts.

claim: Wasmtime is an embeddable runtime for WebAssembly, WASI, and the Component Model.
evidence_quote_or_paraphrase: Wasmtime can be used as a command-line utility or embedded library and targets WebAssembly, WASI, and Component Model.

claim: Wasmtime/WebAssembly sandboxing is import/export based and can prevent raw system access unless the host grants capabilities.
evidence_quote_or_paraphrase: All interaction with the outside world is through imports and exports; there is no raw access to syscalls or I/O. WASI filesystem access follows a capability-based model.
notes: Strong argument for WASM as sandboxed plugin ABI.

claim: WASM determinism is not automatic; host imports and Wasmtime config matter.
evidence_quote_or_paraphrase: Docs say Wasm is mostly deterministic but nondeterminism can enter through imports, NaN values, relaxed SIMD, memory/table growth, and interruption mode.
notes: WASM does not preserve CAK determinism unless configured and constrained.

claim: Wasmtime supports deterministic fuel-based interruption and faster but non-deterministic epoch interruption.
evidence_quote_or_paraphrase: Fuel interruption is deterministic but slower; epoch interruption is faster but non-deterministic and wall-time based.
notes: CAK should use fuel for replay/admission experiments if using WASM.

claim: The Component Model provides richer typed interfaces and WIT worlds/packages for cross-language component boundaries.
evidence_quote_or_paraphrase: Components are self-describing binaries that interact through interfaces; WIT describes imports/exports, interfaces, and worlds.
notes: Good long-term ABI candidate, heavier than JSON string spike.

claim: WASI 0.2 is stable, WASI 0.3 is preview, and several APIs remain proposal-stage.
evidence_quote_or_paraphrase: WASI README describes 0.2 as modular APIs and 0.3 as current preview; proposals list has many APIs in phases 0-3.
notes: Ecosystem maturity is promising but not a reason to force WASM into v0 core.

claim: Extism is designed for WebAssembly plugin systems and can simplify PDK/host integration.
evidence_quote_or_paraphrase: Extism positions one primary use case as extensible software/plugins and untrusted user code; Rust PDK shows plugin_fn exports compiled to wasm32-unknown-unknown.
notes: Good spike candidate; less standards-native than Component Model/WIT.

claim: Agy recommended C-to-B: keep JSON boundary now, target WASM plugin ABI later.
evidence_quote_or_paraphrase: Agy rejected replacing the Rust core, endorsed sandboxed plugin ABI, and warned that forcing WASM now can hurt simple-user DX.

claim: Claude-haiku warned that WASM now risks ABI lock-in before CAK knows what a skill is.
evidence_quote_or_paraphrase: Claude argued not to use WASM as core runtime yet and to revisit Component Model when 3+ ecosystems, versioning, and isolation are proven needs.
